method: orchestrator-direct (278-line self-contained JS diff fully loaded; specialized reviewer rubrics applied inline with empirical verification harnesses instead of subagent fan-out)

acCompliance.evidence: buildFidelityAddition project-scope filter (skill-catalog.mjs:217-222); test skill-catalog.test.mjs:202 + proxy-fidelity.test.mjs:108
full tier byte-identical to pre-0016 verified empirically; proxy-fidelity.test.mjs:123, skill-catalog.test.mjs:214
context-budget-bench.mjs harness; LOCAL_SETUP.md records 2812 tok (full) -> 147 tok (project)
query='' in project scope (skill-catalog.mjs:234); test skill-catalog.test.mjs:220 + proxy-fidelity.test.mjs:148
lean byte-identical verified; tool-compressor.mjs and cli.mjs not in diff (git show --stat)

findings.description: The project-scope allow-filter lowercases both sides ([...projectSkills, ...always].map(s => s.toLowerCase()) and s.name.toLowerCase()), but priority() ranks with a case-SENSITIVE /^sw:/ test and case-sensitive projectSkills.includes(name). A catalog entry differing only in case (e.g. 'SW:DO') would pass the allow-filter yet rank in the lower priority tier and miss project-skill prioritization.
findings.impact: Cosmetic ranking drift only — never fires in practice because Claude Code skill names are always lowercase-namespaced (sw:do). No correctness or cache impact.
findings.recommendation: Optional: make priority()'s sw: test case-insensitive (/^sw:/i) and compare projectSkills case-insensitively for full consistency, or accept as immaterial.

findings.description: _projectSkillMemo never clears in a running proxy (only the test hook _resetProjectSkillMemo does). A skill added under .claude/skills/ mid-session is not picked up until restart.
findings.impact: Intentional per spec AC-US3-01 ('one fs read per project dir, not per request') — staleness is the explicit trade for cacheability. Proxy is a long-running per-project process; restarting on skill changes is acceptable.
findings.recommendation: None required. Note kept for awareness; could add a TTL only if hot-reload is later desired.

findings.description: readProjectSkillNames swallows all fs errors and returns [] (per-entry statSync/existsSync wrapped in try/catch, outer try/catch around readdirSync).
findings.impact: Explicitly specified as best-effort no-throw behavior (AC-US3-01). Not a silent-failure defect: a missing/unreadable project dir legitimately means 'no project skills', and the caller still injects workflow-core. Matches the silent-failures rubric exclusion for intentional graceful degradation.
findings.recommendation: None. Behavior is documented and tested (skill-catalog.test.mjs:193).

securityNotes: No injection/secrets/auth surface. readProjectSkillNames does fs reads under a caller-supplied dir (LOCAL_PROJECT_DIR/cwd) — operator-controlled, local, read-only directory listing; no path-traversal risk from untrusted input. LOCAL_SKILL_ALWAYS env is split/trimmed, used only for in-memory name matching, never executed. No findings.
recommendations: Ship as-is. Zero critical/high/medium findings; all 8 ACs PASS with empirical verification.
Optional cleanup (F-001): make priority() case-insensitive for full consistency — non-blocking.
